description: Make the smoke test script environment-aware and update documentation to cover the new dev environment, lifecycle scripts, and Pulumi stack config approach.

userStories.title: Make smoke test environment-aware
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want the smoke test script to work against any environment so that I can validate both local and remote deployments.

Update monorepo/infra/scripts/smoke-test.sh:
- Accept an optional environment argument (default: local)
- Use appropriate kubeconfig (~/.kube/tdp-<env>.yaml)
- For remote clusters: discover Linode public IP from kubeconfig server URL or Pulumi stack output, use nip.io hostname (smoke.<ip>.nip.io) instead of smoke.localhost, use normal DNS resolution instead of --resolve flag, handle self-signed TLS (--insecure or --cacert for cert-manager certs)
- Update error messages to reference the correct *-up.sh script per environment
- Verify the test works against both local and dev environments
acceptanceCriteria: smoke-test.sh accepts an optional environment argument
Default environment is local when no argument provided
Remote environments use correct kubeconfig path
Remote environments discover Linode IP and use nip.io hostnames
Remote environments handle self-signed TLS certificates
Error messages reference the correct *-up.sh script for the environment
Script works against local environment (existing behavior preserved)
Script works against dev environment with nip.io URLs

userStories.title: Update documentation
userStories.description:
As a developer, I want the infrastructure documentation to cover the dev environment so that contributors understand how to use and extend the multi-environment setup.

Update monorepo/infra/README.md (or CONTRIBUTING.md):
- Add dev environment section alongside local and production
- Document dev-up.sh / dev-down.sh / production-down.sh usage
- Document the Pulumi stack config approach for contributors adding platform charts (read config values, provide sensible defaults, no isLocal checks)
- Document multi-kubeconfig workflow (KUBECONFIG env var or --kubeconfig flag)
- Update environment comparison tables
- Note that dev is fully disposable — no backups, dev-down.sh destroys everything
- Note that k3s uses local-path provisioner (not Linode Block Storage CSI) for persistent volumes — data is lost on node replacement
acceptanceCriteria: README.md documents the dev environment alongside local and production
dev-up.sh and dev-down.sh usage is documented
Pulumi stack config approach is documented for contributors
